title: Tinus divergens
intro: Tinus diversus is a plant species also known as 'Toadflax', a type of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Tinus diversus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures to alleviate symptoms of colds, bronchitis, and other respiratory ailments.
